title: fagara oahuensis
intro: Fagara oahuensis, also known as the Hawaiian prickly ash, is a plant species native to Hawaii and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. This plant contains a high concentration of compounds with medicinal properties, particularly limonoids and flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Fagara oahuensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antiparasitic properties, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Fagara oahuensis can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
